current_ibex_headline.note: Current Ibex transform families are exhausted beyond this accepted lead.

why: Compact single-file Verilog core suitable for fast harness iteration.
Native Makefile includes Yosys and formal flows.
Different implementation style from Ibex while still customer-relevant RISC-V RTL.
first_harness_work: Create selected-flow synth wrapper and stable area/timing report paths.
Emit elaborated source for residual constant-prop filtering.
Run top_level_first.py no-op receipt before testing candidate patches.
risks: Need selected-toolchain timing convention before headline-quality PPA.
Source-only constant-prop volume must be filtered by selected elaboration before spend.

why: Bit-serial RISC-V core gives a sharply different area/timing profile from Ibex.
Good stress test for whether detectors transfer to ultra-small datapaths.
first_harness_work: Use the selected-flow residual detector dry result as a negative transferability receipt.
Try another non-Ibex core or add new detector families before committing formal/toggle spend.
risks: Very small/serial architecture may leave little safe structural slack.
Positive baseline slack means useful area reductions may not move customer-visible timing.

why: Direct Verilog five-stage RISC-V core with a very different timing profile from Ibex and SERV.
Fast selected-flow baseline without a generator stack, useful for ATH-2686 transferability checks.
first_harness_work: Use the selected-flow residual detector dry result as a second negative transferability receipt.
Try CV32E40P selected-flow profiling or new detector families before committing formal/toggle spend.
risks: Core-level baseline has selected-flow timing violations and is not a SoC wrapper.
Source-only parameter leads are consumed by selected-flow elaboration under current detector logic.

why: Configurable pipelined 32-bit RISC-V core with a different frontend/backend split from Ibex.
Customer-relevant class: FPGA-friendly configurable CPU rather than a single fixed public dogfood core.
first_harness_work: Provision Java plus SBT/Mill or import a pinned generated VexRiscv.v before running detectors.
Choose and record the generated Verilog configuration before selected-flow synthesis.
Record generator command, repo commit, generated RTL hash, and tool versions.
Add a no-op top_level_first.py receipt before optimization candidates.
risks: Generator stack may make cold replay heavier than PicoRV32.
Need to pin generated RTL so optimization evidence is not tied to an ambient Scala/Spinal setup.
The checked-in Verilog files are board wrappers or test memory images, not a generated CPU baseline.

why: Customer-relevant OpenHW-class RISC-V core with a larger control/datapath split than Ibex.
SystemVerilog frontend conversion is reproducible with sv2v, so detector evidence can be replayed without an ambient simulator project.
Unlike PicoRV32, SERV, and ultraembedded/riscv under the current families, frontend-filtered detector replay still leaves source-level shared-term leads to profile.
first_harness_work: Treat the load_store_unit shared-term factor as a cheap-kill unless a selected-flow full-core harness shows aggregate movement beyond the bounded neutral profile.
Let the Kairos ATH-2685 formal-context filter remove sleep_unit SVA/property rows before synthesis spend.
Try new detector families or finish a selected-flow full-core harness before committing CV32E40P equivalence/toggle spend.
risks: Full selected-flow mapping exceeded the cheap scout budget during ABC mapping of cv32e40p_mult in the scratch run.
The current receipt is frontend/recon only, not a PPA comparator.
The load_store_unit module profile is generic Yosys only; it is not a full-core selected-flow area/timing comparator.
Sleep-unit source-level shared terms are formal/SVA predicates and require the Kairos formal-context filter before replay.
SystemVerilog conversion uses a lint-wrapper configuration with FPU disabled; alternate configurations need separate receipts.
